page: Hetzner Cloud cpx32 is a virtual server instance powered by the QEMU hypervisor on the x86_64 architecture. It features 4 shared vCPUs derived from AMD EPYC Genoa processors running at a base clock speed of 2.0 GHz. The hardware configuration includes 8.0 GB of system memory, providing 2.0 GB of RAM per core, and is bundled with 160 GB of local SSD storage. The CPU architecture supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512, including AVX-512 VNNI and AVX-512 BF16, alongside cryptographic acceleration via AES and SHA_NI.

In benchmark evaluations, the cpx32 demonstrates top-tier single-core CPU performance, placing in the top 10% of tested instances in stress-ng single-core tests. Multi-core performance falls within the average range, as evidenced by stress-ng multi-core and Geekbench multi-core scores. Memory write bandwidth for large uncached blocks is strong, ranking in the top 25%, while read bandwidth remains average. However, static web serving throughput is a notable bottleneck, registering in the bottom 25% of benchmarks. For lightweight machine learning tasks, the instance achieves average text generation speeds of 15.22 tokens per second on a medium 7B model, though prompt processing for the same model is weak.

Given its shared CPU allocation and 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, the cpx32 offers a cost-efficient entry point for compute-heavy workloads that do not require dedicated physical cores. The combination of high single-core performance and integrated SSD storage makes it suitable for software compilation, text processing, and lightweight database operations. However, the shared CPU model and weak static web serving throughput make it less optimal for high-traffic web hosting or sustained multi-threaded production workloads.
description: Hetzner Cloud cpx32 is an x86_64 virtual server featuring 4 shared AMD EPYC Genoa vCPUs running at 2.0 GHz, paired with 8.0 GB of RAM and 160 GB of local SSD storage. Operating under the QEMU hypervisor, this instance delivers top-tier single-core performance, placing in the top 10% in stress-ng benchmarks, alongside strong memory write bandwidth. Multi-core capabilities and memory read speeds remain average, while static web serving throughput is weak. The CPU supports AVX-512 and cryptographic instructions, enhancing its utility for specialized calculations. With a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, the cpx32 provides a cost-effective profile for compute-focused tasks, software compilation, and small-scale database operations, though its shared CPU allocation presents tradeoffs for sustained multi-threaded workloads.
og_description: Hetzner Cloud cpx32 is an x86_64 virtual server with 4 shared AMD EPYC Genoa vCPUs, 8 GB RAM, and 160 GB SSD storage, offering top-tier single-core performance and average multi-core capabilities.
meta_description: Hetzner Cloud cpx32 features 4 shared AMD EPYC Genoa vCPUs, 8 GB RAM, and 160 GB SSD, delivering top-tier single-core CPU performance.
tagline: A budget-friendly virtual server combining top-tier single-core processor performance with local SSD storage for compute-focused workloads.
